limitations: does not infer business requirements automatically
sandbox hardening is partial: --deny-network blocks Python socket creation but cannot fully confine arbitrary native subprocess behavior
does not replace end-to-end integration tests, coverage.py, mutation testing, or real service/database verification
import-smoke can execute import-time side effects, so use explicit specs for side-effectful projects
supported: import probes executed in subprocesses with timeout
function probes using JSON-serializable args/kwargs and expectations
import-smoke probes for discovered production modules

limitations: requires coverage.py installed
coverage percentages do not prove assertions are meaningful
flaky or environment-specific tests can still distort metrics

dataflow.database_consistency: detects SQLite/Postgres/MySQL marker mismatches in runtime config, env contracts, and simple Python arrays/dicts; does not execute migrations or connect to databases
dataflow.project_call_graph: lightweight static graph with unknown-call accounting

limitations: does not deploy or contact live infrastructure
cannot verify provider firewall/DNS/TLS state
external tools must be installed
outputs are summarized and normalized, not fully reinterpreted
regex matching is lexical and can produce false positives/negatives
does not parse language semantics
secret detection is intentionally conservative by default; broad organization-specific patterns should be supplied explicitly

known_gaps: not a proof of universal precision/recall
synthetic stress does not replace largest real-repo benchmarks
quality-matrix orchestrates gates but does not replace deep external SAST or live e2e

limitations: does not connect to DB
does not execute migrations
does not prove schema equivalence
supported: migration presence when DB config exists

limitations: limited mutant operators
equivalent mutants are possible
large projects require explicit --max-mutants/timeout tuning

neutralized_regression_surfaces.external_normalization: Normalizes supported JSON shapes for Semgrep, Bandit, Ruff and Pyright; it does not guarantee semantic equivalence with the external tools beyond their reported records.
neutralized_regression_surfaces.golden_fixtures: Fixture expectations are regression contracts, not proof of full real-world recall or precision.
neutralized_regression_surfaces.ownership_conflicts: Owner conflict detection is lexical and CODEOWNERS-prefix based; it is a governance smell detector, not an organization chart authority.
neutralized_regression_surfaces.zip_fixture_audit: Duplicate zip entries are allowed only when explicitly intentional by name or marker.

production_claim: Use as a CI helper. Do not treat TOTAL=0 as proof of production readiness.

pytest_audit.coverage_audit: coverage.py line/branch budget execution gate available as a separate command
pytest_audit.coverage_uncovered_files: top uncovered measured files can be reported; not a semantic proof
limitations: does not prove semantic adequacy of assertions
pytest plugin behavior depends on caller flags
flaky-test diagnosis is not implemented
pytest_audit.mutation_lite: conservative mutation smoke gate available as a separate command

limitations: test references are string-validated against tests/ corpus
precision/recall estimates remain human-maintained
rule_quality.status: metadata gate, not measured statistical precision proof

limitations: does not query vulnerability databases unless external pip-audit bridge is installed
does not prove package provenance
does not implement a complete type checker internally
tool availability depends on environment
static annotation heuristics can over/under-count dynamic APIs

quality_gate_limitations.v58_policy_scorecard: Policy, CI profile, changed-files and scorecard gates validate declared contracts and selected scopes; they do not prove arbitrary runtime correctness.
